Interesting! The top charcoal is still there as a 1px object at col 63 (the rightmost cell). When I clicked blue at (26, 2), the top charcoal grew from 1px to 3px (cols 61-63). So clicking blue at rows 24-27 (below upper black bar) moves the top charcoal RIGHT (expanding it from the right edge).
Also, the bottom charcoal moved from cols 10-13 to cols 14-17 (right by 4). Wait, no. Looking at the boundaries:
- Before: bottom charcoal at cols 10-13, tail at cols 8-9
- After: bottom charcoal at cols 14-17, tail at cols 12-13
So clicking blue at (26, 2) moved BOTH charcoals RIGHT:
- Top charcoal: grew from 1px (col 63) to 3px (cols 61-63)
- Bottom charcoal: moved from cols 10-13 to cols 14-17
Wait, that doesn't make sense. The blue at rows 24-27 is below the upper black bar, so it should only affect the top charcoal. But the bottom charcoal also moved.
Actually, looking more carefully:
- Before: bottom charcoal at cols 10-13, tail at cols 8-9
- After: bottom charcoal at cols 14-17, tail at cols 12-13
The bottom charcoal moved right by 4 columns. This is consistent with the movement pattern I observed earlier (4 columns per click for the bottom charcoal).
But I clicked at (26, 2), which is the blue square below the upper black bar. This should only affect the top charcoal, not the bottom charcoal.
Wait, maybe the bottom charcoal moved because of the previous action (MOUSE(row=46, col=2)) which was executed before this Python call. Let me check the history.
Actually, looking at the previous Python call's output:

So the bottom charcoal moved from cols 10-13 to cols 14-17 after clicking at (26, 2). This means clicking blue at rows 24-27 also affects the bottom charcoal.
Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me re-check.
Actually, looking at the segmentation output:

- After: "c: 16px, boundary=[[52, 14], [52, 15], [53, 15], [54, 16]]..." (bottom charcoal at cols 14-17)
So the bottom charcoal did move from cols 10-13 to cols 14-17 after clicking at (26, 2). This is strange because I expected only the top charcoal to be affected.
Wait, maybe I'm wrong about which blue squares affect which charcoals. Let me re-examine:

Actually, wait. The previous action was MOUSE(row=46, col=2), which is the blue square below the lower black bar. This should move the bottom charcoal RIGHT. So the bottom charcoal moved from cols 10-13 to cols 14-17 after the previous action (MOUSE(row=46, col=2)), not after the current action (MOUSE(row=26, 2)).
But the segmentation output shows the bottom charcoal at cols 10-13 before the current action and at cols 14-17 after. This is confusing.
Oh wait, I see the issue. The "Current objects" output was from BEFORE the action (MOUSE(row=26, 2)), and the "After click" output was from AFTER the action. So the bottom charcoal moved from cols 10-13 to cols 14-17 after clicking at (26, 2).
But that doesn't make sense because the previous action (MOUSE(row=46, col=2)) should have already moved the bottom charcoal. Let me check the history more carefully.
Actually, I think the issue is that the "Current objects" output was from the current_frame BEFORE the action, which is the same as the frame after the previous action (MOUSE(row=46, col=2)). So the bottom charcoal was at cols 10-13 after the previous action, and it moved to cols 14-17 after the current action (MOUSE(row=26, 2)).
This means clicking at (26, 2) moved the bottom charcoal from cols 10-13 to cols 14-17. But I expected only the top charcoal to be affected.

  - This is the bottom charcoal at rows 52-57, cols 14-17.
So the bottom charcoal did move from cols 10-13 to cols 14-17 after clicking at (26, 2). This is unexpected.
Hmm, but wait. The previous action was MOUSE(row=46, col=2), which should have moved the bottom charcoal RIGHT. If the bottom charcoal was at cols 6-9 before that action, it would have moved to cols 10-13. Then clicking at (26, 2) moved it to cols 14-17.

Maybe the goal is to move the bottom charcoal to align with the green strip. The green strip is at cols 28-29. If the bottom charcoal is at cols 30-33, the tail would be at cols 28-29. But the bottom charcoal's rightmost position is cols 22-25, so it can't reach cols 30-33.
Hmm, let me try a different approach. Maybe the goal is to move the top charcoal to a specific position. The top charcoal is at cols 61-63 (3px). Let me try moving it right to see if it grows.
Actually, the top charcoal is already at the rightmost position (col 63). Moving it right would expand it further to the right, but there's no space beyond col 63.
Wait, the top charcoal is at cols 61-63, which is 3 pixels. It started at cols 0-63 (64 pixels) after I moved it all the way left. Then clicking at (26, 2) moved it to cols 61-63 (3 pixels). So the top charcoal shrank from 64 pixels to 3 pixels.
This means the top charcoal "unpainted" when I clicked at (26, 2). So clicking blue below the upper black bar moves the top charcoal RIGHT (unpainting it).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Top charcoal at cols 61-63 (3px). Try moving it right to unpaint more.
# Also check bottom charcoal position.
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c':
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Charcoal: {n['pixels']}px, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Move top charcoal right (unpaint)
print("\nMoving top charcoal right...")
for i in range(10):
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 26, 'col': 2}])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    top_c = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] < 20]
    if top_c:
        cols = [p[1] for p in top_c[0]['boundary']]
        print(f"  Click {i+1}: top charcoal cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, {top_c[0]['pixels']}px")
    else:
        print(f"  Click {i+1}: no small charcoal found")
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('game_over'):
        print(f"  Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
